title: Tarik Skubal Proves He's Still the Best Pitcher in the World as Tigers Clinch Series Against Yankees
description: Detroit’s ace dismantled New York’s lineup with a one-hit masterclass, while back-to-back home runs fueled a dominant victory and ignited high-stakes trade deadline speculation surrounding the superstar lefty.
fullText:
There's nothing like a Tarik Skubal start.

You never know exactly what you're going to get, but one thing is certain: don't take your eyes off the game for even a moment.

Skubal and the Tigers rocked the Yankees for the second straight game Tuesday night, clinching the series and earning a chance at a massive road sweep against the best team in the American League. If Detroit finishes the job Wednesday afternoon, it would mark the second time in the month of June the Tigers have swept the top team in the A.L., following their June 1-3 sweep of Tampa Bay.

That conversation can wait until tomorrow, though. Today belongs to the hottest name in Major League Baseball as the trade deadline inches closer.

First, let's talk about the baseball game itself, because that's really what I should be covering. Yet it's impossible to ignore the bigger question that continues to grow after every Skubal start: what does another dominant outing mean for the Tigers' future?

While the Skubal debate will dominate water cooler conversations Wednesday morning, the game itself shouldn't get lost in the discussion.

The Tigers' offense was outstanding. Skubal was even better. The bullpen didn't blow it.

Alright, let's break it down a little more.

Detroit wasted no time setting the tone. In a matchup featuring two young arms in Skubal and Cam Schlittler, it was Kerry Carpenter (13) and Riley Greene (10) who stole the spotlight early, launching back-to-back home runs in the first inning. Greene's blast landed in the second deck, and after a Colt Keith single, Spencer Torkelson (14) said, "Hold my beer," and crushed a two-run homer of his own to give Detroit a commanding 4-0 lead before the Yankees had even recorded the third out.

The bottom of the first made it look like the game could turn into a shootout when Ben Rice connected for his 23rd home run of the season.

He settled in immediately and didn't allow another hit until the sixth inning on a ball that slipped through Zach McKinstry's glove. The play was originally ruled a hit, which would've given Skubal a second earned run, but after a few innings the official scorer changed it to an error on McKinstry. As a result, Skubal finished with just one hit allowed and only one earned run, the solo homer surrendered in the opening inning.

Meanwhile, Detroit's offense continued piling on insurance runs throughout the night.

Greene launched his second home run of the game in the third inning, giving him 11 on the season. Then James Outman blasted his third home run of the year, a three-run shot in the sixth inning that stretched Detroit's lead to 9-1 and effectively put the game out of reach.

As good as the offense was—and on a team desperately searching for offensive consistency as the second half pushes the Tigers closer and closer toward elimination—it was once again Tarik Skubal who stole the show.

Which, at this point, is nothing new.

Skubal finished his evening with six innings of work, allowing one hit, two runs (one earned), while striking out nine and walking nobody. He earned his fourth victory of the season, improving to 4-4 while lowering his ERA to 3.15.

And that's where the trade deadline conversation becomes even more fascinating.

Any team that may have been hesitant to pull the trigger on a blockbuster deal for Skubal only needs to watch a performance like this. If they acquire him, they're getting the best pitcher in the world for the second half of the season.

Every dominant outing only increases Skubal's trade value, giving Detroit even more leverage if it decides to move him before the deadline.

But there's another side to that coin.

String together enough wins like this, and the possibility of Detroit holding onto Skubal becomes more realistic. The American League has been surprisingly weak this season, and with every Tigers victory, the odds of Detroit standing pat instead of selling continue to rise.

Of course, that also raises another concern.

If the Tigers don't trade Skubal and don't find a way back into contention before his contract expires, they could ultimately watch the best pitcher in baseball walk away without receiving anything in return.

Tuesday's win moved Detroit within 12 games of .500 and six games behind the final American League Wild Card spot, with another half-game potentially available depending on the outcome of Tuesday night's Mariners-Angels game.

But it's also another game that forces Tigers fans to ask themselves a difficult question.

Is rooting for wins truly what's best for the organization's long-term future? Or do you ignore the future altogether, enjoy the ride, and hope your team wins every single day like any true fan would?

The Tigers and Yankees wrap up their three-game series Wednesday afternoon at 1:35 p.m. ET as Detroit looks to complete its second sweep this month over the top team in the American League.

title: Islanders losing Anders Lee update arrives on eve of free agency

fullText:
Islanders losing Anders Lee update arrives on eve of free agency originally appeared on The Sporting News. Add The Sporting News as a Preferred Source by clicking here.

Anders Lee has the 'C' on his New York Islanders sweater.

It's unclear, though, whether the pending free agent will actually wear that again.

Lee will become an unrestricted free agent at 12 p.m. ET on July 1.

And after a beginning to an offseason that seemed to include Lee negotiating with the Islanders, no contract has been announced. Nothing is done. Lee appears to be leaving, or at least exploring the open market.

"A hulking winger who makes his living around the net, Lee appears set to move on from the Islanders," The Athletic's Chris Johnston wrote in his latest free agent rankings, in which Lee slots as No. 4. "He's a durable, hard-working player and remains a threat to score on the power play, which accounts for 30 of the 124 goals he scored across the past five seasons. Lee brings leadership intangibles, having worn the 'C' on Long Island, and profiles as a power forward who is tough to contain physically."

MORE: What the Gavin McKenna pick means for the Maple Leafs

Lee was a sixth-round pick by the Islanders in the 2009 NHL Draft who went and played college hockey at Notre Dame before reaching the Islanders.

He has spent parts of 14 NHL seasons with this franchise, appearing in 923 games with 308 goals and 241 assists (549 points).

Lee has played 82 games in three of the past four seasons (and 81 in the other one). He's as reliable as they come for the Islanders.

In 2025-26, he finished with 19 goals, 23 assists and 91 hits.

Lee turns 36 in July. He's heading toward the end of his NHL journey.

And it looks like the finish line might be for a team other than the Islanders.

title: Knicks draft pick Jack Kayil could stay in Germany for 2026-27 season
description: German guard Jack Kayil may remain in Europe for the 2026-27 season rather than join the New York Knicks following the 2026 NBA draft.
fullText:
German guard Jack Kayil may remain in Europe for the 2026-27 season rather than join the New York Knicks following the 2026 NBA draft, according to Ian Begley of SNY.

Kayil was the 39th pick after averaging 12.2 points, 3.6 assists, three rebounds and one steal while shooting 37.7% from the field in 65 games last season, helping Alba Berlin to the German League title. He was named the 2026 Bundesliga Best Young Player.

The 6-foot-3 Kayil said after the draft that his plan was to stay in the United States and play for the Knicks next season. However, with the Knicks facing a financial crunch, the organization may elect to stash him in Germany for one more year.

The Knicks' plan entering free agency is for Jack Kayil to play overseas with his current club in Germany, @IanBegley reports pic.twitter.com/ZDwHCVHJd3

— SNY Knicks (@sny_knicks) June 30, 2026

Kayil was highly touted entering the draft this year because of his versatility and ability to play either guard position. He is a crafty scorer who can get into the paint and finish or pull up in the mid-range or beyond the arc, with a smooth step-back shot.

The 20-year-old has also represented Germany at the international level, including two events with the senior team. He most recently competed in the second window of the 2027 FIBA Basketball World Cup European qualifiers, averaging 11.3 points and five assists in four appearances.

The Knicks were ecstatic to draft Kayil and believe he has a bright future ahead. But his arrival in the United States may be delayed by a year to see additional time in Germany.

fullText:
NEW YORK — The New York Liberty are once again Commissioner’s Cup champions.

That was once again the case Tuesday night as heavily favored New York bested the Las Vegas Aces 93-85. But with the game on the line and the Aces mounting a late 17-point comeback, two-time league MVP Breanna Stewart put on her cape, finishing the fourth period with 5 points, four rebounds and two assists to lead New York to victory.

The Aces fought back to take a 2-point lead in the fourth quarter against the Liberty’s second unit. Once Stewart and Sabrina Ionescu re-entered the game, New York went on a 12-0 run over the next three-plus minutes to regain control.

Her 25 points and 11 rebounds, paired with four assists, powered the Liberty over the Aces to make them the first WNBA team to win two Cup titles. It is Stewart’s second Cup MVP trophy and her third title, after also winning the inaugural Cup with the Seattle Storm in 2021.

Without the league’s leading scorer, A’ja Wilson — who was ruled out with a right leg injury Tuesday morning — it was Las Vegas guard Jackie Young who spearheaded the Aces’ surge, pouring in 10 third-quarter points and 15 

Beyond Stewart’s championship-saving heroics, it was Ionescu whose team- and season-high 26 points helped the Liberty secure their second Commissioner’s Cup championship crown in three years.

The Liberty beat the Aces in the 2023 Cup final behind a 16-point, 15-rebound double-double from MVP Jonquel Jones. Jones. On Tuesday night, Jones finished with 9 points, nine rebounds and three assists.

title: With Toronto in past, what could be Bo Bichette's future in New York?
description: Bo Bichette loved his time in Toronto. He was emotional in his return. Will he leave his mark in New York, as well?
fullText:
NEW YORK — Bo Bichette was trying to treat it like any other game, but the emotions swelled to the surface when he was asked about the love of the Blue Jays fans and then again about his relationship with Vladimir Guerrero Jr.

Up until this season, the Blue Jays organization was all that Bichette had known, from the time he was a touted teenager with flowing locks to one-part of a two-headed monster that helped turn the franchise around and reach the World Series.

On Monday night in a Mets uniform and owner of a three-year, $132 million contract, Bichette soaked in the adoration from the Toronto fanbase.

The home crow at Rogers Centre gave their former shortstop a standing ovation after a tribute video ran with his accomplishments and clips from the beginning of his time to his heroic Game 7 home run in the World Series off the Dodgers' Shohei Ohtani.

And then they rose again as Bichette stepped to the batter's box. The Mets infielder tipped his cap under the coercion from Guerrero from first base, evoking a smile.

"It's nice to know you meant a lot to people," Bichette said after the game. "This city spent a lot to me, spent a lot of years here and grew up here, so it feel good."

Bichette said that Monday presented an opportunity to "reflect on good times" from the early portion of the career, but by the end of the night he had "moved on."

The infielder has no regrets about leaving the Blue Jays, offering that he was hoping for a discussion: "I wanted to feel value," Bichette said. "Things just didn't line up."

That compensation could be exactly what keeps Bichette in New York.

While Bichette signed his deal for three years, he can opt out following this season or next, if he decides.

That would be an expensive risk for Bichette, whose $42 million annual salary is tied for the fourth-largest AAV in Major League Baseball, behind Ohtani ($70 million), Kyle Tucker ($60 million) and Juan Soto ($52 million).

While Bichette would be the top free agent infielder, alongside the likes of Alex Bohm, Gleyber Torres, Luis Arraez and Jazz Chisholm Jr., it would take a major bump in production to raise his earning potential.

After Monday night's loss, Bichette is slashing .251/.296/.383 with 10 home runs, 46 RBI and 44 runs and a 0.0 bWAR.

On top of that, the lingering labor dispute in the offseason between the league and the players union over a new collective bargaining agreement could also be another rationale for Bichette to stay in New York.

This article originally appeared on NorthJersey.com: With Toronto in past, what could be Bo Bichette's future in New York?

title: JetBlue flight hits drone on its way to JFK airport in NYC: FAA
description: The pilot said the jet collided with the drone at approximately 3,000 feet altitude while approaching JFK at around 7:15 a.m. ET.
fullText:
(NewsNation) — A JetBlue pilot reported striking a drone while flying into New York City on Monday morning, federal officials confirmed.

“It hit us right above the cockpit,” the pilot can be heard saying in communications with air traffic controllers.

The pilot said JetBlue flight 948 collided with the drone at approximately 3,000 feet altitude while approaching John F. Kennedy International Airport at around 7:15 a.m. ET., the Federal Aviation Administration wrote on X. The pilot told air traffic control it was about 10-12 miles before landing.

A post-flight inspection didn’t reveal any damage to the plane, but the FAA said it will continue investigating. “This information is preliminary and subject to change,” the agency added.

The plane had departed from Las Vegas before the crew “reported a possible drone encounter during the aircraft’s final approach into New York,” JetBlue wrote in a statement.

“The flight landed without incident, customers deplaned normally, and the plane was removed from service for a post-flight inspection, which found no damage or evidence of a collision,” according to the statement. “Safety is JetBlue’s first priority, and we will assist with any relevant investigations.”
